This is the core querying tool. It takes the service path, entity set, and optional filters to fetch actual data.

# Add this tool to your sap_dynamic_mcp_server.py

@McP.tool()

async def query_any_sap_service(

    service_path: str,

    entity_set: str,

    top: int = 5,

    filters: Optional[str] = None

) -> str:

    """

    Query any SAP OData service dynamically.

    :param service_path: The service name (e.g., 'iwbep/GWSAMPLE_BASIC' or 'sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER')

    :param entity_set: The table/set to query (e.g., 'ProductSet' or 'A_BusinessPartner')

    :param top: The maximum number of records to return.

    :param filters: OData filter syntax (e.g., "City eq 'New York'").

    """

    async with get_client() as client:

        # Construct dynamic URL

        url = f"{config.base_url}/{service_path}/{entity_set}"

       

        params = {

            "$top": str(top),

            "$format": "json"

        }

        if filters:

            params["$filter"] = filters

 

        headers = {"Accept": "application/json"}

       

        try:

            response = await client.get(url, params=params, headers=headers)

           

            if response.status_code != 200:

                return json.dumps({

                    "error": f"SAP error {response.status_code}",

                    "details": response.text[:500]

                })

 

            return json.dumps(response.json(), indent=2)

           

        except Exception as e:

            return json.dumps({"error": f"Request failed: {str(e)}"})

 

  1. Server Runtime

Finally, ensure your server script can be run directly.

# Add this at the very end of your sap_dynamic_mcp_server.py

if __name__ == "__main__":

    mcp.run()

 

Setting up Claude Desktop to Use Your Dynamic Server

Now that your sap_dynamic_mcp_server.py is ready, let's connect it to Claude.

pip install fastmcp httpx

 

Download the Claude Desktop app.

  1. Configure claude_desktop_config.json

This file tells Claude how to launch your Python server. It's usually found at %APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json (on Windows).

Replace your existing mcpServers section with this, ensuring the paths are absolutely correct for your project's virtual environment:

{

  "mcpServers": {

    "sap-odata-dynamic": { # Use a new name to distinguish it

      "command": "C:\\Users\\r_vod\\PycharmProjects\\ClaudeMCP\\.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe",

      "args": [

        "-u",

        "C:\\Users\\r_vod\\PycharmProjects\\ClaudeMCP\\sap_dynamic_mcp_server.py"

      ],

      "env": {

        "PYTHONUTF8": "1"

      }

    }

  }

}

 

  1. Restart Claude Desktop

This is crucial! Claude only reads its configuration at startup.

  1. Go to your Windows System Tray (bottom right, near the clock).
  2. Right-click the Claude icon.
  3. Select Quit.
  4. Relaunch Claude from your Start Menu.
  1. Verify in Claude's Developer Tab
  2. Once Claude restarts, open its settings (Ctrl+,) and navigate to the Developer tab. You should see sap-odata-dynamic listed, ideally with a green "Running" status. If there are errors, check the log files and correct any errors.

 

 

  1. Your First Conversation with the Dynamic Server

Open a new chat in Claude. Now you can use your new intelligent SAP tools!

Example Prompts and MCP Server Claude Responses

 

 

 

 

 

Why This Changes Everything

Instead of retiring old services or getting lost in new ones, you simply ask Claude: "List the available services and tell me which ones handle Sales." The AI does the heavy lifting of discovery, structure inspection, and data retrieval in one seamless flow.

Conclusion

You've now built a powerful, dynamic bridge between Claude AI and your SAP OData services. This setup not only streamlines your development workflow but also empowers anyone using Claude to intuitively explore and extract data from SAP without needing deep technical knowledge of specific service endpoints or complex OData syntax. This is a massive step towards making enterprise data more accessible and usable through natural language

entry.title: Re: Replacing SMTP with Microsoft Graph API for Workflow Email Notifications in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content: @HenrikE, Thanks for your comment. I have checked your reference link but it is giving SMTP config details. We are looking to replace SMTP with MS Graph API.

entry.title: Re: How to pass selectall values of a single selection filter in UrlParameter of SAC?
entry.content: In SAP Analytics Cloud, the “Select All” option isn’t passed as a value — it simply means no restriction is applied. To replicate that when navigating between stories, you should omit the f01Val parameter in your script. Passing a specific value filters to that member, but leaving it out lets the target story load with all members included, which is effectively the same as “Select All.”

"The service cannot be copied because it has been retired and is no longer available for new requests".

If anyone has any insight into this it would be appreciated.

entry.title: Posting not possible in company code 4000 owing to legacy data transfer Message no. AA377
Re: Deep Dive into SAP Datasphere Object Store of BDC: Benefits, Architecture and implementation
entry.content:
Thanks for bringing the topic to the table.

There is something missing though. Where are the numbers that tell about what kind of performance we can expect? And where are the implementation learnings? We initiated our Datasphere project in December and we had a wish / dream that we could use the object store as the primary storage as it is the only place from where you can share to Databricks. I.e. if everything is in object store then we wouldn't have to move data around between spaces in Datasphere. But performance was not acceptable for reporting and it wasn't constant. Also partitioning tables did not change performance which would normally be the case in SPARK when handling parquet files. So there is something not working. Also it turned out that the data types used in replication flows between S/4 and object store wasn't compatible to the data types used in the HANA based spaces. I.e. using the object store for cold data is possible but it takes extra work to cast e.g. all measure data types and again with an unknown performance impact.

The performance of sharing to databricks is not great, again partitioning did not help and it did not matter if you selected 10 columns out of 400 or if you took all 400, which indicates that the benefits of the column based parquet files are not used.  Additionally it is extra work to have to create data products which by design are very rigid, i.e. they may be good in an end scenario where everything is carved in stone, but you will need something else in the development phase. And if you have that something else, would you then spend time on both side handling data products?

SAP should share information about expected performance in some well defined cases, describing files sizes, columns, partioning, scaling of the spark engine etc.

Object store is a great idea but the current implementation is lagging in implementation and performance. 

With kind regards

Kristian Appel

entry.title: Compliance Obligation Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for EHS
entry.content:
What is EHS Compliance Obligation Management?

To understand EHS compliance obligation management, lets first understand EHS Management Systems. EHS management systems is a structured framework of policies, procedures and practices that organizations use to manage risk related to environmental protection, workplace health and safety. Most frameworks follow a “plan”, “do”, “check”, and “act” model. Some of the most common frameworks for EHS management systems are ISO 14001, ISO 45001, EMAS, OSHA VPP, etc. Each of these frameworks commonly list criteria to implement a process to identify, ensure access to, analyze and communicate appliable legal and voluntary obligations. See table below:

 

Standard/Framework

Reference

Key requirement

ISO 14001:2015 (Environmental Management System)

Clause 6.1.3 – Compliance obligations

The organization shall determine and have access to compliance obligations related to its environmental aspects

 

ISO 45001:2018 (Occupational Health & Safety Management System)

Clause 6.1.3 – Determination of legal and other requirements

The organization shall establish, implement, and maintain a process to determine and have access to legal and other requirements related to OH&S hazards and risks.

Responsible Care® and RC14001®

Environment, health, safety, and security code elements

Require identification and evaluation of compliance with applicable laws and regulations

EMAS (EU Eco-Management and Audit Scheme

Annex II, Section A.3.2

Requires evaluation of compliance with relevant environmental legislation

EPA or OSHA based Management System (US)

OSHA VPP, EPA Audit Policy

Compliance assurance is an expected element

 

Objective of the EHS Compliance Obligation Management process is exactly to support a core governance process helping ensure organizations systematically identify, evaluate and maintain conformance with applicable EHS legal and voluntary obligations.

 

Key Features

  • Regulatory obligation content management provisioning (Road Map)
  • Site specific obligation register management
  • AI enabled document centric permit management
  • Applicability assessment
  • Planning, Management and Monitoring of tasks
  • Overview Page with key performance indicators

 

Benefits

  • Translates complex legal requirements into actionable controls
  • Increase operational efficiency by automating workflows and reduce data duplicates
  • Reduces risk of regulatory violations, penalties, or reputational damage
  • Provides evidence of due diligence to regulators, customers, and certification bodies
  • Ensures top management has visibility into compliance performance and risks

 

Next Steps

Use the links below to configure and test the compliance obligation management functionality in Compliance Obligation Management for Environment, Health, and Safety with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

  • Step-by-step configuration guide

 

To follow the full series, subscribe to the SAP Environment, Health, and Safety Management  tag and stay informed about upcoming publications.
